items.title: South Korea's $880 billion chip and AI plan faces big power and water challenges — a single megacluster requires a quarter of Seoul's total power demand
items.description: The ₩1,350 trillion total combines a $520 billion semiconductor program with AI data center and robotics spending, mostly made up of corporate capex.
items.content: Share Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter South Korean President Lee Jae-myung announced a ₩1,350 trillion (roughly $880 billion) 10-year public-private plan for semiconductors, AI data centers, and robotics on June 29. At the televised address in Seoul, he was flanked by Samsung Executive Chairman Lee Jae-yong and SK Group Chairman Chey Tae-won.The ₩1,350 trillion total combines a $520 billion semiconductor program with AI data center and robotics spending, most of it corporate capital expenditure rather than direct state funding. Samsung's Device Solutions division booked ₩53.7 trillion in first-quarter operating profit and expects 2026 to out-earn its entire prior semiconductor history. Samsung and SK hynix have pulled fab completion dates forward by as much as 12 years, while the transmission lines and water pipelines that those fabs depend on remain years behind.Gigawatts of power deficitThe Yongin Semiconductor National Industrial Complex, the Samsung and SK hynix megacluster in Gyeonggi Province, is estimated to require 15 to 16 GW at full operation, close to 25% of total Seoul-metropolitan power demand, against local supply of about 1.9 GW, according to a National Assembly Research Service report. As of a January briefing, however, about 6 GW of the roughly 15 GW the complex needs had no finalized supply plan, with Samsung reportedly needing 9 GW (6 GW secured) and SK hynix 6 GW (3 GW secured).Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: Power in South Korea is generated on the coasts, from nuclear and liquefied natural gas (LNG) on the east coast and renewables in the southwestern Honam region, while the fabs sit inland, near Seoul. Closing that distance falls to state utility KEPCO, which is pursuing a ₩37 trillion, roughly 1,153-km 345 kV network to move east-coast and Honam power to Yongin, targeted for 2036. KEPCO's track record on long-distance lines is problematic, though; its losses from delays on the Bukdangjin-Sintangjeong line reached ₩1.17 trillion ($810 million), and that single project took 22 years to complete, with site selection alone running past a decade.The east-coast link that's supposed to carry about 8 GW toward the capital region has its own history of local opposition, too. The Donghaean-Dongseoul high-voltage direct current line, a 280-km run from Uljin to Hanam requiring 436 towers, has faced repeated delays, and Hanam's 2024 rejection of a KEPCO substation expansion threatened the plan outright. Meanwhile, SK hynix pulled the completion of its fourth Yongin fab forward by 12 years, from 2045 to 2033, as announced in the recent $712.5 billion SK hynix commitment, meaning fabs are arriving faster than the power lines that feed them. You may like South Korea unveils $520 billion investment plan with Samsung and SK Hynix to expand memory chip dominance SK hynix to invest $712.5 billion in South Korean operations Microsoft attributes $25 billion of AI budget to memory and chip costs KEPCO and the government plan six LNG plants inside the Yongin complex, starting at about 3 GW and scaling toward 10 GW, to bridge the supply gap until the transmission network is finished. Both Samsung and SK hynix hold RE100 commitments to reach 100% renewable electricity by 2050, and the ruling Democratic Party's own carbon-neutrality committee has demanded the LNG plan be cancelled.Water shortagesAside from power, a large memory fab consumes upwards of 100,000 tons of water per day, and the Yongin national complex is projected to need around 800,000 tons per day once fully built. The plan to supply this runs in phases: roughly 200,000 tons per day from about 2031, drawn from Paldang Dam surplus and treated wastewater, followed by new intake facilities and pipelines to reach 600,000 tons per day by 2034. However, SK hynix's accelerated fourth fab is now due in 2033, a year before the integrated pipeline that's being built to serve it.Local water disputes have already delayed the buildout, with objections from Yeoju City and Hanam City stalling pipeline permits, and one groundbreaking was cancelled outright. The friction is worse for the new southwestern cluster near Gwangju, where water may prove harder to secure than power, because the Yeongsan and Seomjin river basins hold only about half the water of the Han basin that supplies Yongin. Meanwhile, existing Seomjin and Juam dam supply contracts are already fully allocated, and the four planned southwestern fabs are estimated to need around 430,000 m3 per day of industrial water. Government projections put the Yeongsan basin at an annual shortfall of roughly 219 million m3 by 2030, before any of the fabs draw a single drop.Financed by the memory boomSamsung reported preliminary second-quarter 2026 operating profit of ₩89.4 trillion ($58.4 billion) on ₩171 trillion in revenue on July 7, a roughly 19-fold year-on-year jump and a record for any tech company. Its Device Solutions chip division booked ₩53.7 trillion of the company's ₩57.2 trillion first-quarter operating profit, and DS president Kim Yong-kwan told a July 3 town hall that 2026 chip profit will exceed the cumulative total the division has earned across roughly 40 years in the business. SK hynix, meanwhile, posted a record ₩47.21 trillion operating profit for 2025, overtook Samsung as South Korea's most valuable listed company in June, and filed to raise about $29 billion in a Nasdaq listing whose proceeds are earmarked for Yongin, Cheongju packaging, and EUV tools.Those numbers rest on contract prices that have run sharply higher through 2026, with commodity DRAM up around 90% in the first quarter and 50% to 60% in the second, as memory makers tilted wafer capacity toward HBM. The same dynamic underpins the government's confidence and its exposure. Bank of America has argued that fears of a memory-cycle peak are premature, noting the industrial cluster won't produce meaningful output until 2033 at the earliest. Morgan Stanley cautioned — also on July 7 — that the memory industry is nearing a peak in its rate of change, while stressing that this doesn't indicate a downturn. A plan financed by a price surge inherits the risk that the surge doesn't last until the fabs it funds come online.The plan is ultimately the flagship of a president who took office nine months ago, in a country that removed and jailed his predecessor. Yoon Suk Yeol declared martial law in December 2024, was impeached within days, and was removed by the Constitutional Court the following April. A snap election last June brought Lee Jae-myung to office, and in February this year, Yoon was sentenced to life in prison for insurrection. Industrial policy has held across the turnover, with the Semiconductor Special Act passing the National Assembly with bipartisan support in May, but its dedicated ₩2 trillion account doesn't begin operating until 2027.South Korea caps the workweek at 52 hours, and a proposed exemption for chip R&D staff was cut from the Semiconductor Special Act before passage. Industry and the conservative opposition backed the carve-out, citing TSMC's three-shift operations and longer hours at Chinese competitors, while the ruling party and a coalition of labor organizations opposed it as corporate favoritism.A stopgap now lets firms run R&D staff up to 64 hours per week for limited periods with labor-ministry approval. Samsung's foundry holds about 7% of the contract chipmaking market against TSMC's 72%, and SK hynix supplies most of the HBM feeding Nvidia's AI accelerators, so the plan directs its capital toward memory and leaves Korea's weaker logic position largely untouched. Lee's single five-year term ends in 2030, three years before the plan's fabs are due to reach meaningful output.
items.author: Luke James
items.titleZh: 韩国8800亿美元的芯片和AI计划面临巨大的电力和水挑战——一个超大型集群就需要首尔总电力需求的四分之一
items.descriptionZh:
2025年6月29日，韩国总统李在明在首尔发表电视讲话，宣布了一项为期10年、总额1350万亿韩元（约合8800亿美元）的公共-私营联合投资计划，涵盖半导体、AI数据中心和机器人领域。三星电子执行董事长李在镕和SK集团董事长崔泰源陪同出席，凸显了该计划的国家级战略意义。

该计划中，半导体领域投资约5200亿美元，其余用于AI数据中心和机器人。绝大多数资金来自企业资本支出，而非直接政府拨款。三星设备解决方案（DS）部门2026年第一季度营业利润达53.7万亿韩元，预计全年芯片利润将超过该部门过去约40年的累计总和。SK海力士2025年营业利润创纪录地达到47.21万亿韩元，并在6月超越三星成为韩国市值最高的上市公司，同时提交了约290亿美元的纳斯达克上市申请，募资将用于龙仁、清州封装和EUV设备。这些惊人利润的基础是2026年内存合同价格持续飙升：大宗DRAM第一季度上涨约90%，第二季度上涨50%-60%，主要原因是内存厂商将晶圆产能转向高带宽存储器（HBM）。

然而，这项雄心勃勃的计划面临严重的能源和基础设施瓶颈。根据韩国国会研究服务处的报告，位于京畿道的龙仁半导体国家产业园区（三星和SK海力士的巨型集群）满负荷运营时需要15-16吉瓦（GW）电力，接近首尔都市圈总用电需求的25%，而当地供电能力仅约1.9 GW。截至2026年1月的简报，园区约15 GW需求中仍有约6 GW没有确定的供电方案，其中三星预计需要9 GW（已落实6 GW），SK海力士需要6 GW（已落实3 GW）。韩国电力公司（KEPCO）计划投资37万亿韩元建设约1153公里的345千伏输电网络，将东部沿海的核电和液化天然气（LNG）电力以及西南部全罗道的可再生能源电力输送到龙仁，目标完工时间为2036年。但KEPCO的长距离输电项目记录堪忧：仅北唐津-新堂井一条线路就耗时22年（选址就超过10年），损失达1.17万亿韩元（约8.1亿美元）。另一条从蔚珍到河南的东海-东首尔高压直流线路（280公里，需436座铁塔）也因持续延误和2024年河南市政府拒绝KEPCO变电站扩建计划而面临威胁。与此同时，SK海力士已将其第四座龙仁工厂的完工时间提前了12年，从2045年提前至2033年，意味着芯片工厂的建设速度远快于为其供电的输电线路。为了在输电网络建成前填补供电缺口，KEPCO和政府计划在龙仁园区内建设6座LNG发电厂，初始容量约3 GW，逐步扩展至10 GW。但三星和SK海力士均有RE100承诺（2050年实现100%可再生能源电力），执政的共同民主党自身的碳中和委员会也已要求取消LNG电厂计划。

水资源短缺是另一个严峻挑战。大型内存工厂每天耗水超过10万吨，龙仁园区完全建成后预计每天需水约80万吨。供水方案分阶段实施：约2031年起，每天从八堂大坝余水和处理后的废水中提取约20万吨；到2034年，通过新建取水设施和管道，将日供水量提升至60万吨。然而，SK海力士加速建设的第四工厂计划2033年投产，比面向该工厂的集成管道完工时间早了一年。地方水资源纠纷已导致管道建设延迟：骊州市和河南市的反对使管道许可停滞，甚至有一次奠基仪式被直接取消。在光州附近的新西南产业集群，水资源问题可能比电力更棘手——荣山江和蟾津江流域的水量仅为供应龙仁的汉江流域的一半，而现有的蟾津和珠岩大坝供水合同已满负荷分配。四座计划中的西南部工厂预计每天需要约43万立方米工业用水。政府预测，到2030年，荣山江流域的年缺水量将达约2.19亿立方米，而届时这些工厂尚未运行。

该计划的政治背景同样引人注目。李在明九个月前就任总统，他的前任尹锡悦于2024年12月宣布戒严令，数天内被弹劾，2025年4月被宪法法院罢免，并在2026年2月因内乱罪被判处终身监禁。产业政策在政权交替中保持连贯：《半导体特别法》于2026年5月以跨党派支持通过国民议会，但其专用2万亿韩元账户到2027年才启动。韩国规定每周工作上限为52小时，一项拟议的芯片研发人员豁免条款在法案通过前被删除。行业和保守派反对党支持豁免，援引台积电的三班制和中国竞争对手更长的工作时间；执政党与劳工联盟则反对该条款，认为这是企业优待。目前的一项临时措施允许企业在获得劳动部批准后，在有限时期内将研发人员周工作时间延长至64小时。三星的代工业务仅占合约芯片制造市场约7%（台积电占72%），SK海力士供应着英伟达AI加速器所需的大部分HBM，因此该计划将资本集中于内存领域，而韩国较弱的逻辑芯片制造地位基本未被触及。李在明的五年单届任期将于2030年结束，比计划中芯片工厂实现大规模产出的2033年早了三年。美银分析认为，内存周期见顶的担忧为时过早，因为工业集群最早要到2033年才能产生有意义的产品；摩根士丹利则在2026年7月7日警告内存行业正接近变化率峰值，但并未预示下行。一个由价格飙升资助的计划，其风险在于价格飙升无法持续到其资助的工厂投产之时。

items.title: AI Innovators Adopt NVIDIA Vera — Why Max Single-Threaded CPU at Scale Matters
items.description: Max single-threaded CPUs at scale are a new category of CPUs built for the agentic AI era.  Across the creation and deployment of an agentic system, the CPU is on the critical path for reasoning, response time and learning. CPUs are the processor which executes the work the AI model commands: the tool calling, code […]
items.content: Max single-threaded CPUs at scale are a new category of CPUs built for the agentic AI era. Across the creation and deployment of an agentic system, the CPU is on the critical path for reasoning, response time and learning. CPUs are the processor which executes the work the AI model commands: the tool calling, code execution, data processing, KV-cache and result analysis. For agents in AI factories, speed matters. The faster the CPU can run the tool, the faster the agent can perform the task at hand. For the AI factory, the utilization of GPU is the most valuable resource in the data center so any time waiting for a task to complete constrains the revenue of an AI factory — or worse, impacts the GPU utilization waiting for the CPU to finish its task. AI factories need a CPU with max single-threaded performance to maximize AI factory revenue and agent performance. Today’s data center CPUs are not designed for speed at scale. While the world has fast CPUs for PCs and workstations, data center CPUs have been evolving in directions away from single-threaded performance. The advent of the cloud has pushed CPU makers to build higher core-count CPUs while minimizing cost at the expense of performance. Building CPUs that optimize costs per rentable core increased the number of cores per chip while taking away silicon area from what makes those cores run fast — like high-performance memory fabrics and faster instruction processing per core. The move to chiplet architectures further reduced cost but created a “chiplet tax” where each CPU’s cores can no longer can get access to the full memory performance of the chip. AI agents need a CPU designed for max single-threaded performance at scale. A max single-threaded CPU at scale keeps each agent step fast while the system is fully loaded. Every core completes the agent task at full performance without other cores slowing it down. Max single-threaded CPUs at scale are designed differently to deliver: Strong performance per core under load Enough memory bandwidth per core to keep active cores supplied with data Predictable latency Every core can finish its task without any other core slowing it down, delivering excellent throughput and, more importantly, the fastest possible single-core task performance possible. NVIDIA Vera exemplifies this new class of CPU design. How Max Single-Threaded CPUs at Scale Are Built to Run the Agentic Loop An AI agent doesn’t stop running after a single request. It acts in a loop. The model reasons about the next step. The CPU executes the work around the model. The result comes back. The model decides what to do next. Then the loop runs again. That pattern creates a demand profile for which conventional CPUs were not optimized. Traditional CPU work is intermittent and user-driven, made up of short interactions triggered by people. Agentic work is persistent and parallel: swarms of agents running continuously, each advancing through a chain of steps where each step depends on the result of the one before it. More cores in a CPU means more agent tasks per CPU, and data center CPUs need lots of cores to maximize throughput of tasks. However, adding more cores to a CPU cannot shorten the time for each step inside a single agent loop. More cores can’t make any one task run faster. In fact, CPUs designed to maximize core count can even slow down the performance of each core as they contend for resources. Individual per-core performance matters to drive the speed of each step’s completion. The throughput of additional cores is useful but insufficient. And since each action is dependent on the previous result, per-core speed determines how fast the loop advances. Single-threaded core performance vs. throughput. In the end, the best agentic CPU needs the best single-threaded performance per core, and every core needs to deliver that performance without compromise. The world counts in seconds. Agents count in nanoseconds. NVIDIA Vera is built for this new category — and speed — of work. NVIDIA Vera Is the Max Single-Threaded CPU at Scale for Agents NVIDIA Vera is a max single-threaded CPU at scale, designed from the ground up for the agent loop: the work that happens between model calls as agents use tools, process data, run code and check results. The NVIDIA Vera CPU. At the core of Vera is Olympus, NVIDIA’s custom CPU core, which delivers 50% higher instructions per cycle than NVIDIA Grace. That matters because many agent steps are sequential. A tool call, code execution, test run or data-processing step must finish before the next model call can use the result. Faster cores move each loop forward faster. Vera pairs those faster cores with up to 1.2TB/s of LPDDR5X memory bandwidth at less than 40 watts of memory power, plus a monolithic compute die that helps active cores stay fed and keeps data movement predictable with 3.4TB/s of core-to-core bandwidth, 3x greater than any other data center CPU. This enables all 88 cores with the full memory performance of the CPU without creating bottlenecks that slows down every core. The result is faster agent loops. In loaded CPU workloads that represent agentic execution, Vera delivers 1.8x the sustained per-core performance of x86. Those gains compound across tool calls, code executions, data-processing steps and verification passes, helping AI factories complete more agent work with the GPUs they already operate. Perplexity tested Vera on the agentic work it runs every day. Running a real coding workflow — cloning a repository and running its test suite in sandboxes — Vera completed the job about 1.5x faster than x86, and started concurrent sandboxes up to 1.9x faster. Perplexity is now looking to deploy Vera in its upcoming production system. Agents also depend on data. They query, retrieve, filter and move information constantly, and Vera runs those CPU-side data workloads faster. Partners have measured 3x faster large-scale SQL analytics with Starburst and up to 6x lower latency on real-time streaming with Redpanda, both against leading x86 server CPUs. Agent work isn’t one workload. An agent runs tools and sandboxes, processes data, serves requests and trains the next model with reinforcement learning — and all of it leans on the same strengths. One Vera handles the whole range, rather than requiring a different CPU for each kind of work. And because Vera is the same CPU that hosts the GPUs in NVIDIA Vera Rubin and powers the NVIDIA BlueField-4 STX storage processor, the whole AI factory runs on one architecture and one toolchain. And NVIDIA’s not done. NVIDIA’s next-generation Rosa CPU with the Rigel core will continue the company’s CPU roadmap for the agentic AI era. Rigel is NVIDIA’s next-generation Arm v9.2 CPU core, delivering higher per-core performance than Olympus while keeping the same silicon footprint. Key improvements include better instruction delivery, a larger L2 cache and more efficient memory handling. Built for the Speed of Agents In the agentic AI era, there will be billions of agents, and every one of them will turn to a CPU to act, check, retrieve, execute and verify. In this new market, completed agent work is the product. Faster agent loops help every GPU spend more time generating revenue producing work and less time waiting. NVIDIA Vera is the CPU built for that future. Learn more about the NVIDIA Vera CPU.
items.author: Ian Buck
items.titleZh: AI创新者采用NVIDIA Vera——为何大规模下最大单线程CPU至关重要
items.descriptionZh:
随着AI智能体（agent）进入规模化部署时代，传统数据中心CPU的设计哲学正面临根本性挑战。智能体系统从创建到部署的整个生命周期中，CPU始终处于推理、响应时间和学习的核心路径上——它负责执行AI模型发出的指令：工具调用、代码运行、数据处理、KV-cache管理和结果分析。在AI工厂中，速度决定一切：CPU执行工具越快，智能体完成任务的速度就越快；而GPU利用率是数据中心最宝贵的资源，任何因等待CPU完成任务而造成的GPU空转，都直接压缩AI工厂的营收。因此，AI工厂需要的是一种专为大规模单线程性能优化的CPU，以最大化营收和智能体性能。

现有的数据中心CPU并非为速度而设计。虽然PC和工作站领域拥有高速单线程CPU，但数据中心CPU的发展方向却背离了单线程性能：云计算的到来迫使芯片制造商以牺牲性能为代价，追求更高核心数并降低成本。为优化每个可租用核心的成本，厂商不断增加单芯片核心数，同时削减那些让核心跑得更快的硅面积——比如高性能内存结构和更快的单核指令处理能力。转向小芯片架构进一步降低了成本，却引入了“小芯片税”：每个CPU核心无法再访问全芯片的内存性能。AI智能体需要的，是一种能在满载状态下保持每个智能体步骤快速完成、且每个核心互不干扰的CPU——即“大规模最大单线程CPU”。

**核心技术原理与创新点**

NVIDIA Vera正是这类新型CPU的典范。它围绕智能体循环（agentic loop）从零设计：模型推理下一步，CPU执行模型外围工作，结果返回，模型决策下一步，循环往复。这一模式产生的需求曲线与传统CPU截然不同——传统工作负载是间歇性、用户驱动的短交互，而智能体工作是持续性、并行的：成群的智能体连续运行，每一步都依赖前一步的结果。更多核心意味着每个CPU能承载更多智能体任务，但增加核心数无法缩短单步耗时。事实上，追求最大核心数的CPU反而因资源争用而拖慢每个核心的性能。

Vera的核心是NVIDIA自研的Olympus CPU核心，其每时钟周期指令数（IPC）相比NVIDIA Grace提升50%。这之所以重要，是因为智能体步骤大多是顺序执行的：工具调用、代码执行、测试运行或数据处理必须在上一步完成后才能进入下一轮模型调用，更快的核心意味着每轮循环加速前进。Vera将更快的核心与高达1.2TB/s的LPDDR5X内存带宽（内存功耗低于40瓦）相结合，并采用单片计算die设计，使活动核心始终有数据供应，同时通过3.4TB/s的核心间带宽（是其他数据中心CPU的3倍）保持数据移动的可预测性。这种设计让全部88个核心都能获得CPU的完整内存性能，不会产生任何瓶颈。

**性能参数与对比数据**

在代表智能体执行的高负载CPU工作负载中，Vera的持续单核性能达到x86的1.8倍。这种优势在工具调用、代码执行、数据处理和验证环节中不断累加，帮助AI工厂用现有GPU完成更多智能体工作。

实际测试验证了这一点。Perplexity在每日实际运行的编码工作流中对比测试：克隆仓库并在沙盒中运行测试套件，Vera比x86快约1.5倍，启动并发沙盒的速度甚至快1.9倍。Perplexity计划在即将投产的系统中部署Vera。

智能体工作还高度依赖数据处理——查询、检索、过滤和移动信息是常态。Vera在这些CPU侧数据负载上表现更优：合作伙伴实测显示，使用Starburst进行大规模SQL分析时速度提升3倍，使用Redpanda进行实时流处理时延迟降低6倍（均对比领先的x86服务器CPU）。

更重要的是，智能体工作并非单一负载。一个智能体需要同时运行工具和沙盒、处理数据、服务请求、甚至通过强化学习训练模型——所有这些任务都依赖相同的核心优势。Vera一芯即可处理全范围负载，无需为不同工作类型配备不同CPU。此外，由于Vera同时也是NVIDIA Vera Rubin中搭载GPU的宿主CPU，以及NVIDIA BlueField-4 STX存储处理器的核心，整个AI工厂可运行在统一架构和工具链上。

**技术影响与应用场景**

NVIDIA并未止步于此。其下一代Rosa CPU将搭载Rigel核心，继续推进面向智能体AI时代的CPU路线图。Rigel是NVIDIA下一代Arm v9.2 CPU核心，在保持相同硅面积的前提下提供比Olympus更高的单核性能，关键改进包括更好的指令传输、更大的L2缓存和更高效的内存处理。

在智能体AI时代，我们将迎来数十亿个智能体。每一个智能体都需要借助CPU来执行、检查、检索、运行和验证。完成智能体工作本身就是产品——更快的智能体循环让每个GPU将更多时间用于产生营收的工作，而非等待。NVIDIA Vera正是为这一未来而生的CPU。

items.title: AVX-512 support is reportedly returning with Intel's next-gen Nova Lake CPUs — Latest Linux kernel patches reveal P-cores and E-cores will gain native 512-bit execution
items.description: It looks like Intel is adding back AVX-512 support to its client CPUs starting from the upcoming Nova Lake desktop lineup. Previously, we expected to see AVX-256 debut on a consumer family, allowing E-cores to execute 256-bit code, but now it seems that even the E-cores will gain native 512-bit registers.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 8 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter When Intel switched to a hybrid architecture with its 12th-Gen Alder Lake PUs, it removed AVX-512 support from the lineup entirely because the E-cores didn't support it. Since then, every subsequent generation has shipped without it... until now. Just today, a new Linux patch pushed in the RAID optimized path has revealed that AVX-512 is finally returning to Intel CPUs with Nova Lake, present on both P-cores and E-cores. (Image credit: Future)Intel has been working toward a unified AVX solution for the past few years, as it was originally a champion of the SMID extensions before running into the hybrid hurdle with Alder Lake. Getting past that hurdle is AVX10, which Intel first detailed a few years back. With AVX10.2, 512-bit instructions will run on the P-cores, while either core type can handle converged 256-bit instructions.As such, the E-cores would have their processing width capped at 256-bit, while the P-cores would be open to the full 512-bit wide pipelines. Any thread could swiftly move between either core type with AVX10 implemented. Previously, if the scheduler shifted a 512-bit task running on a P-core to an E-core, the application would crash instantly because those E-cores couldn't process the instruction.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: However, the new patches suggest that Intel has now mandated native 512-bit execution across both P-cores and E-cores, no longer requiring the latter to step down and process the data a bit slower. This is a major development over the standard we originally expected Intel to adopt; the E-cores are apparently becoming just as performant as the P-cores when it comes to SIMD instructions with Nova Lake and later.Intel's original announcement showed that it had already uncoupled the software improvements of AVX-512 from the physical width of the register, so the new instruction features could remain present for both 512-bit and 256-bit execution. This includes things like masking, embedded broadcast for rounding math operations, and doubling the number of the registers themselves from 16 to 32. You may like Intel's Nova Lake CPUs gear up to seize AMD’s 3D V-Cache gaming throne in early leak Intel's Nova Lake lineup to feature a SKU with 12 Xe3P iGPU cores, compete with Ryzen G-series APUs Intel reportedly adding two new 22-core SKUs with game-boosting cache to Nova Lake-S lineup It remains unclear if we will ever see this version of AVX10 on client CPUs, as it seems Nova Lake is going purely for 512-bit execution across both core types. AMD's current-gen Zen 5 processors also have full 512-bit wide registers, while the previous Zen 4 architecture divided a single 512-bit task across two 256-bit execution units over two clock cycles. This ensured execution remained disruption-free even if it took longer. (Image credit: Future)The last time we saw native AVX-512 support on an Intel client family was Rocket Lake (11th Gen), right before the hybrid era ushered in by Alder Lake. For modern AI workloads and other compute-heavy tasks such as encoding or simulations, AVX-512 instructions bring a huge performance benefit that's foolish to be left on the table. Keep in mind that this is just a Linux patch at the moment and that Intel hasn't officially announced native AVX-512 support for Nova Lake yet.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.

items.titleZh: 据报道，英特尔的下一代Nova Lake CPU将重新支持AVX-512 —— 最新的Linux内核补丁显示，P核和E核将获得原生512位执行能力。
items.descriptionZh:
英特尔在12代酷睿Alder Lake转向混合架构（P核+E核）时，因E核不支持AVX-512指令集，不得不从整个产品线中移除了该功能。此后的每一代酷睿处理器都沿用了这一妥协，直到现在。最新披露的Linux内核补丁显示，随着下一代Nova Lake处理器的到来，AVX-512将正式回归，并且同时覆盖P核和E核，实现原生512位指令执行。这一变化标志着英特尔在SIMD（单指令多数据流）扩展上的重大战略转向。

**背景与上下文**：AVX-512是英特尔为高性能计算和AI工作负载设计的512位宽SIMD指令集，能显著加速矩阵运算、编码、科学模拟等任务。然而，自12代Alder Lake引入大小核混合架构后，由于E核（能效核）的物理寄存器宽度仅为256位，无法直接处理512位指令，英特尔被迫在消费级CPU中完全禁用AVX-512。后续Raptor Lake、Meteor Lake等代际均延续了这一限制，使得英特尔在AI加速与高吞吐计算上落后于对手。作为对比，AMD的Zen 4架构采用将单个512位任务拆分为两个256位操作、两个时钟周期完成的方式实现兼容，而Zen 5则直接配备了全512位宽寄存器。

**核心技术原理与创新点**：英特尔此前提出的AVX10方案试图解决这一矛盾——通过“统一AVX”解耦指令集功能与寄存器物理宽度。按照原始规划，AVX10.2版本允许P核运行完整的512位指令，而E核则处理收敛后的256位指令，且线程可在两类核心间迁移。但问题在于，若调度器将一个正在P核上执行的512位任务迁移到E核，缺乏硬件支持的E核会直接导致应用崩溃。新补丁彻底改变了这一设计：英特尔强制要求Nova Lake的P核与E核均原生支持512位执行宽度，E核不再需要降级处理。这意味着E核在SIMD密集型任务中拥有了与P核相同的吞吐能力，消除了混合架构下指令集兼容性的最大瓶颈。

**性能参数与对比数据**：原生512位寄存器带来的直接优势是单指令周期内可处理的数据量翻倍（相比256位），尤其适合AI推理、视频编码、物理仿真等并行计算场景。与AMD Zen 4相比，Zen 4虽能运行AVX-512，但通过双256位单元拼接实现，实际吞吐约为原生512位的70-80%，且存在额外延迟；而Nova Lake的E核若真的实现原生512位，则性能可完全对齐P核，甚至优于Zen 4的实现。此外，补丁暗示英特尔已进一步将寄存器数量从16个翻倍至32个，并保留了掩码运算、嵌入式广播等高级特性，这些都是在AVX10框架下独立于物理宽度的软件优化。不过目前仍不清楚Nova Lake是否会同时保留AVX10的256位收敛模式，因为补丁显示其完全转向了原生512位执行。

**技术影响与应用场景**：AVX-512的回归对消费级CPU意义重大。自Rocket Lake（11代）之后，英特尔桌面平台已有四年缺乏原生AVX-512支持。随着AI工作负载（如本地大语言模型推理、Stable Diffusion图像生成）逐渐向客户端转移，缺乏512位指令集导致英特尔的CPU在AI加速上依赖额外硬件（如NPU）或效率较低的矢量扩展。Nova Lake重新提供完整AVX-512后，用户可直接在CPU上获得接近专用加速器的吞吐提升。在专业领域，视频转码（如H.265/HEVC编码）、科学计算（有限元分析、分子动力学）、数据压缩与加密等任务均能受益。此外，由于E核也具备原生512位能力，混合架构下的任务调度将不再需要规避AVX-512指令，操作系统和软件生态的兼容性将大幅简化。

**现状与展望**：目前这一信息仅来源于Linux内核补丁中的“RAID优化路径”，英特尔尚未官方确认Nova Lake的AVX-512支持细节。考虑到消费级CPU的功耗和散热限制，E核实现完整512位宽度对芯片面积和能效的挑战不容忽视，不排除最终产品采用混合策略（如E核仍为256位但通过优化调度避免崩溃）的可能。无论如何，这一补丁表明英特尔已下定决心在下一代产品中解决混合架构的SIMD兼容性短板，以应对AMD和ARM阵营在AI加速领域的竞争。对于追求极致计算性能的桌面发烧友和开发者而言，这无疑是2025年最值得期待的技术革新之一。

items.title: Intel patent reveals new XBM memory architecture that ditches HBM's costly silicon interposer — backend-transistor DRAM stack uses UCIe links and built-in repair to ease AI's memory bottleneck
items.description: Intel’s XBM patent proposes an HBM alternative that uses backend-transistor DRAM, UCIe chiplet links, and repair logic to reduce packaging costs and complexity.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 2 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter An Intel patent application published on July 2, 2026, surfaced by Underfox, has revealed the company's plans for a new high-bandwidth memory (HBM) architecture that aims to ease the packaging and cost bottleneck of today's interposer-based HBM. The patent application — filed back on December 26, 2024 — describes what Intel calls cross-batch memory (XBM), an "ultra-high-bandwidth memory with backend transistors" built with the goal of matching HBM4's footprint while swapping conventional DRAM and its ultra-wide interface for back-end-of-line (BEOL) transistors and serial Universal Chiplet Interconnect Express (UCIe) links.Intel's proposed design is a memory stack that addresses the assembly costs that make conventional HBM expensive by dropping the costly silicon interposer and shrinking the package, while building in its own defect repair. Package cross-section showing the HBM stack (104) and logic die (106) on an interposer. (Image credit: Intel)The filing lays out a stack of memory dies, each holding one-transistor one-capacitor (1T1C) DRAM fabricated in the back-end-of-line, wired together with through-silicon via (TSV) "gutters" and both-sided high-bandwidth interconnect (HBI) connections. Intel describes dies of roughly 1.5 gigabytes (GB) apiece — 768 "datablocks" arranged in a 32-by-24 grid, grouped into eight channels of eight sub-channels each — stacked eight high and scaling to 16. Data then leaves the stack over UCIe I/O bundles running at 32 gigatransfers per second (GT/s), funneled out through a base die.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: To understand what Intel is changing, it helps to recall what standard high-bandwidth memory does. HBM stacks DRAM dies vertically on a base logic die, threads them together with TSVs, and communicates with the processor across a silicon interposer using an extremely wide parallel interface — on the order of 1,024 bits per stack. This width is how HBM delivers its bandwidth, but it is also what makes it expensive to package and hard to scale, as every one of those wires has to be routed through an interposer sitting between the memory and the compute die. As AI accelerators have outrun the rate at which memory can feed them, this "memory wall" has become the dominant constraint on performance, which is why nearly every large chipmaker is now attacking the interface and the stack rather than the logic.XBM's first major change is structural. Conventional DRAM cells are built in the front-end-of-line, the base silicon layer where transistors are normally fabricated. XBM instead moves the 1T1C cell into the back-end-of-line, the metal-and-via stack above the transistor layer, using thin-film transistors. Building memory in the BEOL is what lets Intel pack the die into many small, independently addressable memory blocks, and it is the same backend-transistor direction Intel has pursued for placing memory directly over logic. You may like Qualcomm reveals HBC near-memory AI architecture, AI250 and AI350 accelerators SK hynix unveils 'iHBM' thermal architecture that cools AI memory at the source NEO Semiconductor's revolutionary 3D X-DRAM for AI processors has passed proof-of-concept validation Angled view of the die stack, showing aligned data blocks and TSVs across layers. (Image credit: Intel)The second change is the interface. Rather than HBM's wide parallel PHY, XBM serializes data onto UCIe bundles at 32 GT/s, with the base die handling the serialize/deserialize step and routing all I/O to the compute die. Moving to a standard chiplet interconnect is what makes the design "chiplet-native" and, Intel argues, simpler and cheaper to package than an interposer-bound HBM stack. The tradeoff is that 32 GT/s is UCIe's current top data rate, so the interface is already running at the spec ceiling rather than leaving obvious headroom.Intel also leans heavily on repairability. The base die carries dedicated spare channels, built-in self-repair (BISR), decode and debug logic, and four sub-channels of redundant memory arrays that act as fungible spares for defects in the dies above — post-assembly repair designed to claw back yield on a very tall stack.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Base die floorplan labeling the UCIe block, BISR/decode/debug region, and spare channels for repair. (Image credit: Intel)A large portion of the patent application focuses not on the memory cell at all but on how to mount it. Intel details memory-on-package (MoP) and "reversed overhang" structures aimed at cutting the stack's Z-height — conventional MoP can add 300 to 350 micrometers (µm) — while removing the stiffener normally needed to control warpage and feeding DRAM power directly from the voltage regulator. This is the concrete basis for the "smaller, cheaper package" claim. Memory-on-package cross-section with die stacks flanking the SoC module (Image credit: Intel)XBM should not be confused with ZAM (Z-Angle Memory), the architecture Intel is co-developing with SoftBank subsidiary SAIMEMORY and set to present at the VLSI Symposium 2026. ZAM's innovation is on the bonding side — a fusion-bonded, nine-layer stack of largely conventional DRAM with roughly 3-µm-thin silicon between tiers — and it reportedly targets around twice HBM4's bandwidth density, with commercialization aimed at 2029. XBM, by contrast, is an Intel-only filing that changes the DRAM transistor itself and the interface. Read together, they suggest Intel is running at least two parallel HBM alternatives, a fitting move for a company that began in 1968 as a memory maker.The caveats on Intel’s proposed HBM architecture are the usual ones for a patent. The patent was filed 18 months ago, and there’s currently no product or roadmap, signaling potential intent rather than a shipping part. The UCIe interface is already at its rate ceiling, backend-transistor DRAM remains unproven at manufacturing scale, and the whole thing still has to justify itself against HBM4E and Intel's own ZAM timeline. Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.

items.titleZh: 英特尔专利揭示全新XBM内存架构，摒弃HBM昂贵硅中介层——后端晶体管DRAM堆叠采用UCIe连接与内置修复技术，缓解AI内存瓶颈
items.descriptionZh:
英特尔一项于2026年7月2日公开的专利申请（由Underfox曝光）揭示了该公司在高带宽内存（HBM）领域的新架构方案，旨在解决当前基于硅中介层的HBM在封装和成本上的瓶颈。该专利于2024年12月26日提交，描述了英特尔称之为“跨批次内存”（Cross-Batch Memory, XBM）的设计——一种“配备后道晶体管的超高带宽内存”，其目标是匹配HBM4的封装尺寸，但用后道（BEOL）晶体管和串行通用小芯片互连标准（UCIe）链路替代传统DRAM及其超宽接口。

英特尔提出的设计是一个内存堆叠架构，通过去掉昂贵的硅中介层并缩小封装体积，同时内置缺陷修复机制，从而解决传统HBM因组装成本过高的问题。专利申请中展示了一个包含内存堆叠和逻辑芯片、坐落于中介层上的封装横截面示意图。具体而言，该方案由多层内存芯片堆叠而成，每层芯片包含在后道工艺中制造的单晶体管单电容（1T1C）DRAM单元，通过硅通孔（TSV）“导流槽”和双面高带宽互连（HBI）连接。英特尔描述了每颗芯片容量约为1.5 GB——由768个“数据块”排列成32×24网格，分为8个通道，每个通道再分为8个子通道——典型堆叠高度为8层，并可扩展到16层。数据通过运行在32 GT/s（每秒千兆传输）的UCIe I/O束从堆叠中输出，经基die汇聚。

要理解英特尔的变革，需先回顾标准HBM的工作原理。HBM将DRAM芯片垂直堆叠在基die上，通过TSV互连，并利用极宽的并行接口（每堆叠约1024位）经由硅中介层与处理器通信。这种宽度赋予了HBM高带宽，但也导致封装昂贵且难以扩展，因为每一条导线都必须通过位于内存与计算芯片之间的中介层布线。随着AI加速器的性能增长远超内存供给速率，这一“内存墙”已成为性能的主导约束，因此几乎所有大型芯片制造商都在攻击接口和堆叠本身，而非逻辑单元。

XBM的第一个重大变化是结构性的。传统DRAM单元制造于前道（FEOL），即晶体管通常所在的基础硅层。而XBM将1T1C单元移至后道（BEOL）——晶体管层上方的金属和通孔堆叠层中，采用薄膜晶体管。在BEOL中构建内存使得英特尔能够将芯片划分为大量独立寻址的小内存块，这与英特尔一贯将内存直接置于逻辑上方的后道晶体管方向一致。

第二个变化是接口。不同于HBM的宽并行PHY，XBM将数据串行化为32 GT/s的UCIe束，由基die处理串并转换并路由所有I/O到计算芯片。采用标准小芯片互连使得设计成为“小芯片原生”的，英特尔认为这比基于中介层的HBM堆叠更简单、更便宜。代价是32 GT/s已是UCIe当前最高数据速率，因此接口已运行在规格上限，而非留有明显余量。

英特尔还高度重视可修复性。基die包含专用备用通道、内置自修复（BISR）、解码与调试逻辑，以及四个子通道的冗余内存阵列，作为上方芯片缺陷的通用备用——这是一种旨在从很高堆叠中挽回良率的组装后修复机制。专利中展示了基die的平面布局图，标注了UCIe模块、BISR/解码/调试区域以及用于修复的备用通道。

专利申请中很大篇幅并非聚焦内存单元本身，而是如何安装它。英特尔详细介绍了内存封装（Memory-on-Package, MoP）和“反向悬垂”结构，旨在降低堆叠的Z轴高度——传统MoP会增加300至350微米——同时去除通常用于控制翘曲的加强件，并直接从电压调节器为DRAM供电。这是“更小、更便宜封装”说法的具体依据。示意图显示了SoC模块两侧的内存die堆叠横截面。

XBM不应与ZAM（Z-Angle Memory）混淆，后者是英特尔与软银子公司SAIMEMORY合作开发、将在2026年VLSI研讨会上展示的架构。ZAM的创新在于键合侧——采用融合键合的九层堆叠，主要由传统DRAM构成，层间硅厚度约3微米——据称其带宽密度约为HBM4的两倍，计划2029年商业化。相比之下，XBM是英特尔独家的申请，更改了DRAM晶体管本身和接口。综合来看，这表明英特尔至少并行推进两条HBM替代路线，这对于一家1968年以内存制造商起家的公司来说是合理的举措。

对英特尔这一HBM架构的谨慎态度一如专利常见情况。该专利提交于18个月前，目前没有产品或路线图，表明这更多是意向而非成品。UCIe接口已达速率上限，后道晶体管DRAM尚未在制造规模上得到验证，整个方案仍需在HBM4E以及英特尔自己的ZAM时间表面前证明自己。

items.title: NVIDIA and Hugging Face Bring New Models and Frameworks to LeRobot for the Open Robotics Community
items.description: Open source AI has shown how quickly developers can innovate when models, data and tools are shared. Robotics has the same opportunity, but advancements in physical AI development can still be gated by costly and fragmented resources, from large datasets and robot foundation models to simulation, compute and validation tools. NVIDIA and Hugging Face are […]
items.content: Open source AI has shown how quickly developers can innovate when models, data and tools are shared. Robotics has the same opportunity, but advancements in physical AI development can still be gated by costly and fragmented resources, from large datasets and robot foundation models to simulation, compute and validation tools. NVIDIA and Hugging Face are collaborating to bring the NVIDIA Isaac GR00T 1.7 open, reasoning vision language action (VLA) model for humanoid robots and the NVIDIA Isaac Teleop framework to LeRobot — Hugging Face’s open source library for robotics — with NVIDIA Cosmos 3, a frontier model for physical AI, planned soon. Together, these integrations give developers a more accessible and standardized path for end-to-end robot development while driving innovation and collaboration across the open robotics community. “Open source is how a field turns advanced research into something people can study, adapt and build on,” said Thomas Wolf, cofounder and chief science officer at Hugging Face. “With NVIDIA Isaac GR00T 1.7 and Isaac TeleOp in LeRobot today, robotics developers can use shared models, data and workflows to train and evaluate robots in the open. And with NVIDIA Cosmos 3 planned next, the community will have a path to bring frontier world models into that same collaborative loop.” An Open Pipeline for Robot Foundation Models Hugging Face LeRobot is an open source robotics library for training, running and sharing robot datasets, models, policies and workflows. NVIDIA’s continued partnership with Hugging Face connects NVIDIA’s 3 million robotics developers with Hugging Face’s 16 million AI builders, expanding access to frontier physical AI tools through open workflows. Bringing NVIDIA physical AI capabilities into LeRobot gives developers a common way to collect and standardize data, train and fine-tune robot foundation models, evaluate performance and deploy models through open workflows. The integrations include: NVIDIA Isaac Teleop, an open source framework for robot data collection, helps developers capture high-quality human demonstrations from external devices using standardized, interoperable formats, then expand and share datasets with the community, all directly in LeRobot. NVIDIA Isaac GR00T 1.7, the first open and commercially viable robot foundation model, makes it easier to post-train and deploy models through LeRobot workflows, helping developers adapt GR00T to new robot embodiments and tasks with benchmarked performance. NVIDIA Cosmos 3, a frontier world foundation model for physical AI coming soon to LeRobot, will help developers generate and augment robotics data, simulate scenarios and support policy development when real-world data is limited or too expensive to collect. These integrations build on a broader set of NVIDIA resources already connected to LeRobot to support the full robotics development loop, including: The largest open source physical AI dataset, downloaded more than 15 million times, which includes more than 350,000 real and simulated trajectories and 57 million grasps to help developers kickstart their robotics workflows. NVIDIA Isaac Sim– and Isaac Lab-based simulation frameworks to help developers set up environments, generate robot data, test policies and validate behaviors before moving to physical robots. NVIDIA Isaac Lab-Arena in LeRobot Environment Hub to enable developers to quickly prototype complex simulation environments, register them in LeRobot EnvHub and seamlessly use them within the LeRobot ecosystem to train and evaluate generalist robot policies such as GR00T, Pi and SmolVLA. NVIDIA Jetson Thor integration with LeRobot’s Reachy 2 to support deployment of VLA models on open source humanoid robots. Learn more about how to use Isaac Teleop, Isaac GR00T 1.7 and Isaac Lab-Arena with LeRobot for end-to-end humanoid development and explore detailed LeRobot integration workflows.
items.author: Sasa Docca
items.titleZh: NVIDIA 和 Hugging Face 为开源机器人社区 LeRobot 带来新模型与框架
items.descriptionZh:
开源AI已经展示了当模型、数据和工具被共享时，开发者能够以多快的速度进行创新。机器人领域同样拥有这样的机遇，但物理AI的进展仍然可能受限于昂贵且碎片化的资源，包括大型数据集、机器人基础模型、仿真、计算和验证工具等。NVIDIA与Hugging Face正在合作，将NVIDIA Isaac GR00T 1.7——一个用于人形机器人的开放推理视觉语言动作（VLA）模型——以及NVIDIA Isaac Teleop框架引入LeRobot——Hugging Face的开源机器人库。此外，NVIDIA Cosmos 3——一个用于物理AI的前沿模型——也计划很快加入。这些集成共同为开发者提供了一条更易访问、更标准化的端到端机器人开发路径，同时推动开源机器人社区的创新与协作。“开源是一个领域将前沿研究转化为可学习、可适配、可构建之物的方式。”Hugging Face联合创始人兼首席科学官Thomas Wolf表示，“通过今天在LeRobot中集成的NVIDIA Isaac GR00T 1.7和Isaac TeleOp，机器人开发者可以利用共享的模型、数据和工作流，以开放的方式训练和评估机器人。而随着NVIDIA Cosmos 3的后续加入，社区将有一条将前沿世界模型引入同一协作循环的路径。”

### 机器人基础模型的开放流水线

Hugging Face LeRobot是一个开源的机器人库，用于训练、运行和共享机器人数据集、模型、策略和工作流。NVIDIA与Hugging Face的持续合作将NVIDIA的300万机器人开发者与Hugging Face的1600万AI构建者连接起来，通过开放工作流扩大对前沿物理AI工具的访问。将NVIDIA的物理AI能力引入LeRobot，为开发者提供了一种通用方式，用于收集和标准化数据、训练和微调机器人基础模型、评估性能以及通过开放工作流部署模型。具体集成包括：

- **NVIDIA Isaac Teleop**：一个用于机器人数据采集的开源框架，帮助开发者从外部设备使用标准化、可互操作的格式捕获高质量的人类演示数据，然后直接在LeRobot中扩展并与社区共享数据集。
- **NVIDIA Isaac GR00T 1.7**：首个开放且具有商业可行性的机器人基础模型，通过LeRobot工作流简化了模型的后训练和部署，帮助开发者将GR00T适配到新的机器人本体和任务上，并提供基准测试的性能。
- **NVIDIA Cosmos 3**：一个即将加入LeRobot的用于物理AI的前沿世界基础模型，将帮助开发者在真实世界数据有限或采集成本过高时生成和增强机器人数据、仿真场景并支持策略开发。

这些集成建立在已与LeRobot连接的更广泛的NVIDIA资源基础之上，以支持完整的机器人开发循环，包括：
- 最大的开源物理AI数据集，已被下载超过1500万次，包含超过35万条真实和仿真轨迹以及5700万次抓取，帮助开发者快速启动机器人工作流。
- 基于NVIDIA Isaac Sim和Isaac Lab的仿真框架，帮助开发者设置环境、生成机器人数据、测试策略并在实际部署前验证行为。
- NVIDIA Isaac Lab-Arena（位于LeRobot环境中心），使开发者能够快速构建复杂仿真环境原型，在LeRobot EnvHub中注册，并无缝地在LeRobot生态系统中用于训练和评估通用机器人策略（如GR00T、Pi和SmolVLA）。
- NVIDIA Jetson Thor与LeRobot的Reachy 2集成，支持在开源人形机器人上部署VLA模型。

了解更多关于如何使用Isaac Teleop、Isaac GR00T 1.7和Isaac Lab-Arena与LeRobot进行端到端人形机器人开发的信息，并探索详细的LeRobot集成工作流。

history.title: South Korea's $880 billion chip and AI plan faces big power and water challenges — a single megacluster requires a quarter of Seoul's total power demand
history.description: The ₩1,350 trillion total combines a $520 billion semiconductor program with AI data center and robotics spending, mostly made up of corporate capex.
history.content: Share Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter South Korean President Lee Jae-myung announced a ₩1,350 trillion (roughly $880 billion) 10-year public-private plan for semiconductors, AI data centers, and robotics on June 29. At the televised address in Seoul, he was flanked by Samsung Executive Chairman Lee Jae-yong and SK Group Chairman Chey Tae-won.The ₩1,350 trillion total combines a $520 billion semiconductor program with AI data center and robotics spending, most of it corporate capital expenditure rather than direct state funding. Samsung's Device Solutions division booked ₩53.7 trillion in first-quarter operating profit and expects 2026 to out-earn its entire prior semiconductor history. Samsung and SK hynix have pulled fab completion dates forward by as much as 12 years, while the transmission lines and water pipelines that those fabs depend on remain years behind.Gigawatts of power deficitThe Yongin Semiconductor National Industrial Complex, the Samsung and SK hynix megacluster in Gyeonggi Province, is estimated to require 15 to 16 GW at full operation, close to 25% of total Seoul-metropolitan power demand, against local supply of about 1.9 GW, according to a National Assembly Research Service report. As of a January briefing, however, about 6 GW of the roughly 15 GW the complex needs had no finalized supply plan, with Samsung reportedly needing 9 GW (6 GW secured) and SK hynix 6 GW (3 GW secured).Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: Power in South Korea is generated on the coasts, from nuclear and liquefied natural gas (LNG) on the east coast and renewables in the southwestern Honam region, while the fabs sit inland, near Seoul. Closing that distance falls to state utility KEPCO, which is pursuing a ₩37 trillion, roughly 1,153-km 345 kV network to move east-coast and Honam power to Yongin, targeted for 2036. KEPCO's track record on long-distance lines is problematic, though; its losses from delays on the Bukdangjin-Sintangjeong line reached ₩1.17 trillion ($810 million), and that single project took 22 years to complete, with site selection alone running past a decade.The east-coast link that's supposed to carry about 8 GW toward the capital region has its own history of local opposition, too. The Donghaean-Dongseoul high-voltage direct current line, a 280-km run from Uljin to Hanam requiring 436 towers, has faced repeated delays, and Hanam's 2024 rejection of a KEPCO substation expansion threatened the plan outright. Meanwhile, SK hynix pulled the completion of its fourth Yongin fab forward by 12 years, from 2045 to 2033, as announced in the recent $712.5 billion SK hynix commitment, meaning fabs are arriving faster than the power lines that feed them. You may like South Korea unveils $520 billion investment plan with Samsung and SK Hynix to expand memory chip dominance SK hynix to invest $712.5 billion in South Korean operations Microsoft attributes $25 billion of AI budget to memory and chip costs KEPCO and the government plan six LNG plants inside the Yongin complex, starting at about 3 GW and scaling toward 10 GW, to bridge the supply gap until the transmission network is finished. Both Samsung and SK hynix hold RE100 commitments to reach 100% renewable electricity by 2050, and the ruling Democratic Party's own carbon-neutrality committee has demanded the LNG plan be cancelled.Water shortagesAside from power, a large memory fab consumes upwards of 100,000 tons of water per day, and the Yongin national complex is projected to need around 800,000 tons per day once fully built. The plan to supply this runs in phases: roughly 200,000 tons per day from about 2031, drawn from Paldang Dam surplus and treated wastewater, followed by new intake facilities and pipelines to reach 600,000 tons per day by 2034. However, SK hynix's accelerated fourth fab is now due in 2033, a year before the integrated pipeline that's being built to serve it.Local water disputes have already delayed the buildout, with objections from Yeoju City and Hanam City stalling pipeline permits, and one groundbreaking was cancelled outright. The friction is worse for the new southwestern cluster near Gwangju, where water may prove harder to secure than power, because the Yeongsan and Seomjin river basins hold only about half the water of the Han basin that supplies Yongin. Meanwhile, existing Seomjin and Juam dam supply contracts are already fully allocated, and the four planned southwestern fabs are estimated to need around 430,000 m3 per day of industrial water. Government projections put the Yeongsan basin at an annual shortfall of roughly 219 million m3 by 2030, before any of the fabs draw a single drop.Financed by the memory boomSamsung reported preliminary second-quarter 2026 operating profit of ₩89.4 trillion ($58.4 billion) on ₩171 trillion in revenue on July 7, a roughly 19-fold year-on-year jump and a record for any tech company. Its Device Solutions chip division booked ₩53.7 trillion of the company's ₩57.2 trillion first-quarter operating profit, and DS president Kim Yong-kwan told a July 3 town hall that 2026 chip profit will exceed the cumulative total the division has earned across roughly 40 years in the business. SK hynix, meanwhile, posted a record ₩47.21 trillion operating profit for 2025, overtook Samsung as South Korea's most valuable listed company in June, and filed to raise about $29 billion in a Nasdaq listing whose proceeds are earmarked for Yongin, Cheongju packaging, and EUV tools.Those numbers rest on contract prices that have run sharply higher through 2026, with commodity DRAM up around 90% in the first quarter and 50% to 60% in the second, as memory makers tilted wafer capacity toward HBM. The same dynamic underpins the government's confidence and its exposure. Bank of America has argued that fears of a memory-cycle peak are premature, noting the industrial cluster won't produce meaningful output until 2033 at the earliest. Morgan Stanley cautioned — also on July 7 — that the memory industry is nearing a peak in its rate of change, while stressing that this doesn't indicate a downturn. A plan financed by a price surge inherits the risk that the surge doesn't last until the fabs it funds come online.The plan is ultimately the flagship of a president who took office nine months ago, in a country that removed and jailed his predecessor. Yoon Suk Yeol declared martial law in December 2024, was impeached within days, and was removed by the Constitutional Court the following April. A snap election last June brought Lee Jae-myung to office, and in February this year, Yoon was sentenced to life in prison for insurrection. Industrial policy has held across the turnover, with the Semiconductor Special Act passing the National Assembly with bipartisan support in May, but its dedicated ₩2 trillion account doesn't begin operating until 2027.South Korea caps the workweek at 52 hours, and a proposed exemption for chip R&D staff was cut from the Semiconductor Special Act before passage. Industry and the conservative opposition backed the carve-out, citing TSMC's three-shift operations and longer hours at Chinese competitors, while the ruling party and a coalition of labor organizations opposed it as corporate favoritism.A stopgap now lets firms run R&D staff up to 64 hours per week for limited periods with labor-ministry approval. Samsung's foundry holds about 7% of the contract chipmaking market against TSMC's 72%, and SK hynix supplies most of the HBM feeding Nvidia's AI accelerators, so the plan directs its capital toward memory and leaves Korea's weaker logic position largely untouched. Lee's single five-year term ends in 2030, three years before the plan's fabs are due to reach meaningful output.
history.author: Luke James
history.titleZh: 韩国8800亿美元的芯片和AI计划面临巨大的电力和水挑战——一个超大型集群就需要首尔总电力需求的四分之一
history.descriptionZh:
2025年6月29日，韩国总统李在明在首尔发表电视讲话，宣布了一项为期10年、总额1350万亿韩元（约合8800亿美元）的公共-私营联合投资计划，涵盖半导体、AI数据中心和机器人领域。三星电子执行董事长李在镕和SK集团董事长崔泰源陪同出席，凸显了该计划的国家级战略意义。

该计划中，半导体领域投资约5200亿美元，其余用于AI数据中心和机器人。绝大多数资金来自企业资本支出，而非直接政府拨款。三星设备解决方案（DS）部门2026年第一季度营业利润达53.7万亿韩元，预计全年芯片利润将超过该部门过去约40年的累计总和。SK海力士2025年营业利润创纪录地达到47.21万亿韩元，并在6月超越三星成为韩国市值最高的上市公司，同时提交了约290亿美元的纳斯达克上市申请，募资将用于龙仁、清州封装和EUV设备。这些惊人利润的基础是2026年内存合同价格持续飙升：大宗DRAM第一季度上涨约90%，第二季度上涨50%-60%，主要原因是内存厂商将晶圆产能转向高带宽存储器（HBM）。

然而，这项雄心勃勃的计划面临严重的能源和基础设施瓶颈。根据韩国国会研究服务处的报告，位于京畿道的龙仁半导体国家产业园区（三星和SK海力士的巨型集群）满负荷运营时需要15-16吉瓦（GW）电力，接近首尔都市圈总用电需求的25%，而当地供电能力仅约1.9 GW。截至2026年1月的简报，园区约15 GW需求中仍有约6 GW没有确定的供电方案，其中三星预计需要9 GW（已落实6 GW），SK海力士需要6 GW（已落实3 GW）。韩国电力公司（KEPCO）计划投资37万亿韩元建设约1153公里的345千伏输电网络，将东部沿海的核电和液化天然气（LNG）电力以及西南部全罗道的可再生能源电力输送到龙仁，目标完工时间为2036年。但KEPCO的长距离输电项目记录堪忧：仅北唐津-新堂井一条线路就耗时22年（选址就超过10年），损失达1.17万亿韩元（约8.1亿美元）。另一条从蔚珍到河南的东海-东首尔高压直流线路（280公里，需436座铁塔）也因持续延误和2024年河南市政府拒绝KEPCO变电站扩建计划而面临威胁。与此同时，SK海力士已将其第四座龙仁工厂的完工时间提前了12年，从2045年提前至2033年，意味着芯片工厂的建设速度远快于为其供电的输电线路。为了在输电网络建成前填补供电缺口，KEPCO和政府计划在龙仁园区内建设6座LNG发电厂，初始容量约3 GW，逐步扩展至10 GW。但三星和SK海力士均有RE100承诺（2050年实现100%可再生能源电力），执政的共同民主党自身的碳中和委员会也已要求取消LNG电厂计划。

水资源短缺是另一个严峻挑战。大型内存工厂每天耗水超过10万吨，龙仁园区完全建成后预计每天需水约80万吨。供水方案分阶段实施：约2031年起，每天从八堂大坝余水和处理后的废水中提取约20万吨；到2034年，通过新建取水设施和管道，将日供水量提升至60万吨。然而，SK海力士加速建设的第四工厂计划2033年投产，比面向该工厂的集成管道完工时间早了一年。地方水资源纠纷已导致管道建设延迟：骊州市和河南市的反对使管道许可停滞，甚至有一次奠基仪式被直接取消。在光州附近的新西南产业集群，水资源问题可能比电力更棘手——荣山江和蟾津江流域的水量仅为供应龙仁的汉江流域的一半，而现有的蟾津和珠岩大坝供水合同已满负荷分配。四座计划中的西南部工厂预计每天需要约43万立方米工业用水。政府预测，到2030年，荣山江流域的年缺水量将达约2.19亿立方米，而届时这些工厂尚未运行。

该计划的政治背景同样引人注目。李在明九个月前就任总统，他的前任尹锡悦于2024年12月宣布戒严令，数天内被弹劾，2025年4月被宪法法院罢免，并在2026年2月因内乱罪被判处终身监禁。产业政策在政权交替中保持连贯：《半导体特别法》于2026年5月以跨党派支持通过国民议会，但其专用2万亿韩元账户到2027年才启动。韩国规定每周工作上限为52小时，一项拟议的芯片研发人员豁免条款在法案通过前被删除。行业和保守派反对党支持豁免，援引台积电的三班制和中国竞争对手更长的工作时间；执政党与劳工联盟则反对该条款，认为这是企业优待。目前的一项临时措施允许企业在获得劳动部批准后，在有限时期内将研发人员周工作时间延长至64小时。三星的代工业务仅占合约芯片制造市场约7%（台积电占72%），SK海力士供应着英伟达AI加速器所需的大部分HBM，因此该计划将资本集中于内存领域，而韩国较弱的逻辑芯片制造地位基本未被触及。李在明的五年单届任期将于2030年结束，比计划中芯片工厂实现大规模产出的2033年早了三年。美银分析认为，内存周期见顶的担忧为时过早，因为工业集群最早要到2033年才能产生有意义的产品；摩根士丹利则在2026年7月7日警告内存行业正接近变化率峰值，但并未预示下行。一个由价格飙升资助的计划，其风险在于价格飙升无法持续到其资助的工厂投产之时。

history.title: AI Innovators Adopt NVIDIA Vera — Why Max Single-Threaded CPU at Scale Matters
history.description: Max single-threaded CPUs at scale are a new category of CPUs built for the agentic AI era.  Across the creation and deployment of an agentic system, the CPU is on the critical path for reasoning, response time and learning. CPUs are the processor which executes the work the AI model commands: the tool calling, code […]
history.content: Max single-threaded CPUs at scale are a new category of CPUs built for the agentic AI era. Across the creation and deployment of an agentic system, the CPU is on the critical path for reasoning, response time and learning. CPUs are the processor which executes the work the AI model commands: the tool calling, code execution, data processing, KV-cache and result analysis. For agents in AI factories, speed matters. The faster the CPU can run the tool, the faster the agent can perform the task at hand. For the AI factory, the utilization of GPU is the most valuable resource in the data center so any time waiting for a task to complete constrains the revenue of an AI factory — or worse, impacts the GPU utilization waiting for the CPU to finish its task. AI factories need a CPU with max single-threaded performance to maximize AI factory revenue and agent performance. Today’s data center CPUs are not designed for speed at scale. While the world has fast CPUs for PCs and workstations, data center CPUs have been evolving in directions away from single-threaded performance. The advent of the cloud has pushed CPU makers to build higher core-count CPUs while minimizing cost at the expense of performance. Building CPUs that optimize costs per rentable core increased the number of cores per chip while taking away silicon area from what makes those cores run fast — like high-performance memory fabrics and faster instruction processing per core. The move to chiplet architectures further reduced cost but created a “chiplet tax” where each CPU’s cores can no longer can get access to the full memory performance of the chip. AI agents need a CPU designed for max single-threaded performance at scale. A max single-threaded CPU at scale keeps each agent step fast while the system is fully loaded. Every core completes the agent task at full performance without other cores slowing it down. Max single-threaded CPUs at scale are designed differently to deliver: Strong performance per core under load Enough memory bandwidth per core to keep active cores supplied with data Predictable latency Every core can finish its task without any other core slowing it down, delivering excellent throughput and, more importantly, the fastest possible single-core task performance possible. NVIDIA Vera exemplifies this new class of CPU design. How Max Single-Threaded CPUs at Scale Are Built to Run the Agentic Loop An AI agent doesn’t stop running after a single request. It acts in a loop. The model reasons about the next step. The CPU executes the work around the model. The result comes back. The model decides what to do next. Then the loop runs again. That pattern creates a demand profile for which conventional CPUs were not optimized. Traditional CPU work is intermittent and user-driven, made up of short interactions triggered by people. Agentic work is persistent and parallel: swarms of agents running continuously, each advancing through a chain of steps where each step depends on the result of the one before it. More cores in a CPU means more agent tasks per CPU, and data center CPUs need lots of cores to maximize throughput of tasks. However, adding more cores to a CPU cannot shorten the time for each step inside a single agent loop. More cores can’t make any one task run faster. In fact, CPUs designed to maximize core count can even slow down the performance of each core as they contend for resources. Individual per-core performance matters to drive the speed of each step’s completion. The throughput of additional cores is useful but insufficient. And since each action is dependent on the previous result, per-core speed determines how fast the loop advances. Single-threaded core performance vs. throughput. In the end, the best agentic CPU needs the best single-threaded performance per core, and every core needs to deliver that performance without compromise. The world counts in seconds. Agents count in nanoseconds. NVIDIA Vera is built for this new category — and speed — of work. NVIDIA Vera Is the Max Single-Threaded CPU at Scale for Agents NVIDIA Vera is a max single-threaded CPU at scale, designed from the ground up for the agent loop: the work that happens between model calls as agents use tools, process data, run code and check results. The NVIDIA Vera CPU. At the core of Vera is Olympus, NVIDIA’s custom CPU core, which delivers 50% higher instructions per cycle than NVIDIA Grace. That matters because many agent steps are sequential. A tool call, code execution, test run or data-processing step must finish before the next model call can use the result. Faster cores move each loop forward faster. Vera pairs those faster cores with up to 1.2TB/s of LPDDR5X memory bandwidth at less than 40 watts of memory power, plus a monolithic compute die that helps active cores stay fed and keeps data movement predictable with 3.4TB/s of core-to-core bandwidth, 3x greater than any other data center CPU. This enables all 88 cores with the full memory performance of the CPU without creating bottlenecks that slows down every core. The result is faster agent loops. In loaded CPU workloads that represent agentic execution, Vera delivers 1.8x the sustained per-core performance of x86. Those gains compound across tool calls, code executions, data-processing steps and verification passes, helping AI factories complete more agent work with the GPUs they already operate. Perplexity tested Vera on the agentic work it runs every day. Running a real coding workflow — cloning a repository and running its test suite in sandboxes — Vera completed the job about 1.5x faster than x86, and started concurrent sandboxes up to 1.9x faster. Perplexity is now looking to deploy Vera in its upcoming production system. Agents also depend on data. They query, retrieve, filter and move information constantly, and Vera runs those CPU-side data workloads faster. Partners have measured 3x faster large-scale SQL analytics with Starburst and up to 6x lower latency on real-time streaming with Redpanda, both against leading x86 server CPUs. Agent work isn’t one workload. An agent runs tools and sandboxes, processes data, serves requests and trains the next model with reinforcement learning — and all of it leans on the same strengths. One Vera handles the whole range, rather than requiring a different CPU for each kind of work. And because Vera is the same CPU that hosts the GPUs in NVIDIA Vera Rubin and powers the NVIDIA BlueField-4 STX storage processor, the whole AI factory runs on one architecture and one toolchain. And NVIDIA’s not done. NVIDIA’s next-generation Rosa CPU with the Rigel core will continue the company’s CPU roadmap for the agentic AI era. Rigel is NVIDIA’s next-generation Arm v9.2 CPU core, delivering higher per-core performance than Olympus while keeping the same silicon footprint. Key improvements include better instruction delivery, a larger L2 cache and more efficient memory handling. Built for the Speed of Agents In the agentic AI era, there will be billions of agents, and every one of them will turn to a CPU to act, check, retrieve, execute and verify. In this new market, completed agent work is the product. Faster agent loops help every GPU spend more time generating revenue producing work and less time waiting. NVIDIA Vera is the CPU built for that future. Learn more about the NVIDIA Vera CPU.
history.author: Ian Buck
history.titleZh: AI创新者采用NVIDIA Vera——为何大规模下最大单线程CPU至关重要
history.descriptionZh:
随着AI智能体（agent）进入规模化部署时代，传统数据中心CPU的设计哲学正面临根本性挑战。智能体系统从创建到部署的整个生命周期中，CPU始终处于推理、响应时间和学习的核心路径上——它负责执行AI模型发出的指令：工具调用、代码运行、数据处理、KV-cache管理和结果分析。在AI工厂中，速度决定一切：CPU执行工具越快，智能体完成任务的速度就越快；而GPU利用率是数据中心最宝贵的资源，任何因等待CPU完成任务而造成的GPU空转，都直接压缩AI工厂的营收。因此，AI工厂需要的是一种专为大规模单线程性能优化的CPU，以最大化营收和智能体性能。

现有的数据中心CPU并非为速度而设计。虽然PC和工作站领域拥有高速单线程CPU，但数据中心CPU的发展方向却背离了单线程性能：云计算的到来迫使芯片制造商以牺牲性能为代价，追求更高核心数并降低成本。为优化每个可租用核心的成本，厂商不断增加单芯片核心数，同时削减那些让核心跑得更快的硅面积——比如高性能内存结构和更快的单核指令处理能力。转向小芯片架构进一步降低了成本，却引入了“小芯片税”：每个CPU核心无法再访问全芯片的内存性能。AI智能体需要的，是一种能在满载状态下保持每个智能体步骤快速完成、且每个核心互不干扰的CPU——即“大规模最大单线程CPU”。

**核心技术原理与创新点**

NVIDIA Vera正是这类新型CPU的典范。它围绕智能体循环（agentic loop）从零设计：模型推理下一步，CPU执行模型外围工作，结果返回，模型决策下一步，循环往复。这一模式产生的需求曲线与传统CPU截然不同——传统工作负载是间歇性、用户驱动的短交互，而智能体工作是持续性、并行的：成群的智能体连续运行，每一步都依赖前一步的结果。更多核心意味着每个CPU能承载更多智能体任务，但增加核心数无法缩短单步耗时。事实上，追求最大核心数的CPU反而因资源争用而拖慢每个核心的性能。

Vera的核心是NVIDIA自研的Olympus CPU核心，其每时钟周期指令数（IPC）相比NVIDIA Grace提升50%。这之所以重要，是因为智能体步骤大多是顺序执行的：工具调用、代码执行、测试运行或数据处理必须在上一步完成后才能进入下一轮模型调用，更快的核心意味着每轮循环加速前进。Vera将更快的核心与高达1.2TB/s的LPDDR5X内存带宽（内存功耗低于40瓦）相结合，并采用单片计算die设计，使活动核心始终有数据供应，同时通过3.4TB/s的核心间带宽（是其他数据中心CPU的3倍）保持数据移动的可预测性。这种设计让全部88个核心都能获得CPU的完整内存性能，不会产生任何瓶颈。

**性能参数与对比数据**

在代表智能体执行的高负载CPU工作负载中，Vera的持续单核性能达到x86的1.8倍。这种优势在工具调用、代码执行、数据处理和验证环节中不断累加，帮助AI工厂用现有GPU完成更多智能体工作。

实际测试验证了这一点。Perplexity在每日实际运行的编码工作流中对比测试：克隆仓库并在沙盒中运行测试套件，Vera比x86快约1.5倍，启动并发沙盒的速度甚至快1.9倍。Perplexity计划在即将投产的系统中部署Vera。

智能体工作还高度依赖数据处理——查询、检索、过滤和移动信息是常态。Vera在这些CPU侧数据负载上表现更优：合作伙伴实测显示，使用Starburst进行大规模SQL分析时速度提升3倍，使用Redpanda进行实时流处理时延迟降低6倍（均对比领先的x86服务器CPU）。

更重要的是，智能体工作并非单一负载。一个智能体需要同时运行工具和沙盒、处理数据、服务请求、甚至通过强化学习训练模型——所有这些任务都依赖相同的核心优势。Vera一芯即可处理全范围负载，无需为不同工作类型配备不同CPU。此外，由于Vera同时也是NVIDIA Vera Rubin中搭载GPU的宿主CPU，以及NVIDIA BlueField-4 STX存储处理器的核心，整个AI工厂可运行在统一架构和工具链上。

**技术影响与应用场景**

NVIDIA并未止步于此。其下一代Rosa CPU将搭载Rigel核心，继续推进面向智能体AI时代的CPU路线图。Rigel是NVIDIA下一代Arm v9.2 CPU核心，在保持相同硅面积的前提下提供比Olympus更高的单核性能，关键改进包括更好的指令传输、更大的L2缓存和更高效的内存处理。

在智能体AI时代，我们将迎来数十亿个智能体。每一个智能体都需要借助CPU来执行、检查、检索、运行和验证。完成智能体工作本身就是产品——更快的智能体循环让每个GPU将更多时间用于产生营收的工作，而非等待。NVIDIA Vera正是为这一未来而生的CPU。

history.title: AVX-512 support is reportedly returning with Intel's next-gen Nova Lake CPUs — Latest Linux kernel patches reveal P-cores and E-cores will gain native 512-bit execution
history.description: It looks like Intel is adding back AVX-512 support to its client CPUs starting from the upcoming Nova Lake desktop lineup. Previously, we expected to see AVX-256 debut on a consumer family, allowing E-cores to execute 256-bit code, but now it seems that even the E-cores will gain native 512-bit registers.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 8 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter When Intel switched to a hybrid architecture with its 12th-Gen Alder Lake PUs, it removed AVX-512 support from the lineup entirely because the E-cores didn't support it. Since then, every subsequent generation has shipped without it... until now. Just today, a new Linux patch pushed in the RAID optimized path has revealed that AVX-512 is finally returning to Intel CPUs with Nova Lake, present on both P-cores and E-cores. (Image credit: Future)Intel has been working toward a unified AVX solution for the past few years, as it was originally a champion of the SMID extensions before running into the hybrid hurdle with Alder Lake. Getting past that hurdle is AVX10, which Intel first detailed a few years back. With AVX10.2, 512-bit instructions will run on the P-cores, while either core type can handle converged 256-bit instructions.As such, the E-cores would have their processing width capped at 256-bit, while the P-cores would be open to the full 512-bit wide pipelines. Any thread could swiftly move between either core type with AVX10 implemented. Previously, if the scheduler shifted a 512-bit task running on a P-core to an E-core, the application would crash instantly because those E-cores couldn't process the instruction.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: However, the new patches suggest that Intel has now mandated native 512-bit execution across both P-cores and E-cores, no longer requiring the latter to step down and process the data a bit slower. This is a major development over the standard we originally expected Intel to adopt; the E-cores are apparently becoming just as performant as the P-cores when it comes to SIMD instructions with Nova Lake and later.Intel's original announcement showed that it had already uncoupled the software improvements of AVX-512 from the physical width of the register, so the new instruction features could remain present for both 512-bit and 256-bit execution. This includes things like masking, embedded broadcast for rounding math operations, and doubling the number of the registers themselves from 16 to 32. You may like Intel's Nova Lake CPUs gear up to seize AMD’s 3D V-Cache gaming throne in early leak Intel's Nova Lake lineup to feature a SKU with 12 Xe3P iGPU cores, compete with Ryzen G-series APUs Intel reportedly adding two new 22-core SKUs with game-boosting cache to Nova Lake-S lineup It remains unclear if we will ever see this version of AVX10 on client CPUs, as it seems Nova Lake is going purely for 512-bit execution across both core types. AMD's current-gen Zen 5 processors also have full 512-bit wide registers, while the previous Zen 4 architecture divided a single 512-bit task across two 256-bit execution units over two clock cycles. This ensured execution remained disruption-free even if it took longer. (Image credit: Future)The last time we saw native AVX-512 support on an Intel client family was Rocket Lake (11th Gen), right before the hybrid era ushered in by Alder Lake. For modern AI workloads and other compute-heavy tasks such as encoding or simulations, AVX-512 instructions bring a huge performance benefit that's foolish to be left on the table. Keep in mind that this is just a Linux patch at the moment and that Intel hasn't officially announced native AVX-512 support for Nova Lake yet.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
history.author: Hassam Nasir
history.titleZh: 据报道，英特尔的下一代Nova Lake CPU将重新支持AVX-512 —— 最新的Linux内核补丁显示，P核和E核将获得原生512位执行能力。
history.descriptionZh:
英特尔在12代酷睿Alder Lake转向混合架构（P核+E核）时，因E核不支持AVX-512指令集，不得不从整个产品线中移除了该功能。此后的每一代酷睿处理器都沿用了这一妥协，直到现在。最新披露的Linux内核补丁显示，随着下一代Nova Lake处理器的到来，AVX-512将正式回归，并且同时覆盖P核和E核，实现原生512位指令执行。这一变化标志着英特尔在SIMD（单指令多数据流）扩展上的重大战略转向。

**背景与上下文**：AVX-512是英特尔为高性能计算和AI工作负载设计的512位宽SIMD指令集，能显著加速矩阵运算、编码、科学模拟等任务。然而，自12代Alder Lake引入大小核混合架构后，由于E核（能效核）的物理寄存器宽度仅为256位，无法直接处理512位指令，英特尔被迫在消费级CPU中完全禁用AVX-512。后续Raptor Lake、Meteor Lake等代际均延续了这一限制，使得英特尔在AI加速与高吞吐计算上落后于对手。作为对比，AMD的Zen 4架构采用将单个512位任务拆分为两个256位操作、两个时钟周期完成的方式实现兼容，而Zen 5则直接配备了全512位宽寄存器。

**核心技术原理与创新点**：英特尔此前提出的AVX10方案试图解决这一矛盾——通过“统一AVX”解耦指令集功能与寄存器物理宽度。按照原始规划，AVX10.2版本允许P核运行完整的512位指令，而E核则处理收敛后的256位指令，且线程可在两类核心间迁移。但问题在于，若调度器将一个正在P核上执行的512位任务迁移到E核，缺乏硬件支持的E核会直接导致应用崩溃。新补丁彻底改变了这一设计：英特尔强制要求Nova Lake的P核与E核均原生支持512位执行宽度，E核不再需要降级处理。这意味着E核在SIMD密集型任务中拥有了与P核相同的吞吐能力，消除了混合架构下指令集兼容性的最大瓶颈。

**性能参数与对比数据**：原生512位寄存器带来的直接优势是单指令周期内可处理的数据量翻倍（相比256位），尤其适合AI推理、视频编码、物理仿真等并行计算场景。与AMD Zen 4相比，Zen 4虽能运行AVX-512，但通过双256位单元拼接实现，实际吞吐约为原生512位的70-80%，且存在额外延迟；而Nova Lake的E核若真的实现原生512位，则性能可完全对齐P核，甚至优于Zen 4的实现。此外，补丁暗示英特尔已进一步将寄存器数量从16个翻倍至32个，并保留了掩码运算、嵌入式广播等高级特性，这些都是在AVX10框架下独立于物理宽度的软件优化。不过目前仍不清楚Nova Lake是否会同时保留AVX10的256位收敛模式，因为补丁显示其完全转向了原生512位执行。

**技术影响与应用场景**：AVX-512的回归对消费级CPU意义重大。自Rocket Lake（11代）之后，英特尔桌面平台已有四年缺乏原生AVX-512支持。随着AI工作负载（如本地大语言模型推理、Stable Diffusion图像生成）逐渐向客户端转移，缺乏512位指令集导致英特尔的CPU在AI加速上依赖额外硬件（如NPU）或效率较低的矢量扩展。Nova Lake重新提供完整AVX-512后，用户可直接在CPU上获得接近专用加速器的吞吐提升。在专业领域，视频转码（如H.265/HEVC编码）、科学计算（有限元分析、分子动力学）、数据压缩与加密等任务均能受益。此外，由于E核也具备原生512位能力，混合架构下的任务调度将不再需要规避AVX-512指令，操作系统和软件生态的兼容性将大幅简化。

**现状与展望**：目前这一信息仅来源于Linux内核补丁中的“RAID优化路径”，英特尔尚未官方确认Nova Lake的AVX-512支持细节。考虑到消费级CPU的功耗和散热限制，E核实现完整512位宽度对芯片面积和能效的挑战不容忽视，不排除最终产品采用混合策略（如E核仍为256位但通过优化调度避免崩溃）的可能。无论如何，这一补丁表明英特尔已下定决心在下一代产品中解决混合架构的SIMD兼容性短板，以应对AMD和ARM阵营在AI加速领域的竞争。对于追求极致计算性能的桌面发烧友和开发者而言，这无疑是2025年最值得期待的技术革新之一。

history.title: Intel patent reveals new XBM memory architecture that ditches HBM's costly silicon interposer — backend-transistor DRAM stack uses UCIe links and built-in repair to ease AI's memory bottleneck
history.description: Intel’s XBM patent proposes an HBM alternative that uses backend-transistor DRAM, UCIe chiplet links, and repair logic to reduce packaging costs and complexity.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 2 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter An Intel patent application published on July 2, 2026, surfaced by Underfox, has revealed the company's plans for a new high-bandwidth memory (HBM) architecture that aims to ease the packaging and cost bottleneck of today's interposer-based HBM. The patent application — filed back on December 26, 2024 — describes what Intel calls cross-batch memory (XBM), an "ultra-high-bandwidth memory with backend transistors" built with the goal of matching HBM4's footprint while swapping conventional DRAM and its ultra-wide interface for back-end-of-line (BEOL) transistors and serial Universal Chiplet Interconnect Express (UCIe) links.Intel's proposed design is a memory stack that addresses the assembly costs that make conventional HBM expensive by dropping the costly silicon interposer and shrinking the package, while building in its own defect repair. Package cross-section showing the HBM stack (104) and logic die (106) on an interposer. (Image credit: Intel)The filing lays out a stack of memory dies, each holding one-transistor one-capacitor (1T1C) DRAM fabricated in the back-end-of-line, wired together with through-silicon via (TSV) "gutters" and both-sided high-bandwidth interconnect (HBI) connections. Intel describes dies of roughly 1.5 gigabytes (GB) apiece — 768 "datablocks" arranged in a 32-by-24 grid, grouped into eight channels of eight sub-channels each — stacked eight high and scaling to 16. Data then leaves the stack over UCIe I/O bundles running at 32 gigatransfers per second (GT/s), funneled out through a base die.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: To understand what Intel is changing, it helps to recall what standard high-bandwidth memory does. HBM stacks DRAM dies vertically on a base logic die, threads them together with TSVs, and communicates with the processor across a silicon interposer using an extremely wide parallel interface — on the order of 1,024 bits per stack. This width is how HBM delivers its bandwidth, but it is also what makes it expensive to package and hard to scale, as every one of those wires has to be routed through an interposer sitting between the memory and the compute die. As AI accelerators have outrun the rate at which memory can feed them, this "memory wall" has become the dominant constraint on performance, which is why nearly every large chipmaker is now attacking the interface and the stack rather than the logic.XBM's first major change is structural. Conventional DRAM cells are built in the front-end-of-line, the base silicon layer where transistors are normally fabricated. XBM instead moves the 1T1C cell into the back-end-of-line, the metal-and-via stack above the transistor layer, using thin-film transistors. Building memory in the BEOL is what lets Intel pack the die into many small, independently addressable memory blocks, and it is the same backend-transistor direction Intel has pursued for placing memory directly over logic. You may like Qualcomm reveals HBC near-memory AI architecture, AI250 and AI350 accelerators SK hynix unveils 'iHBM' thermal architecture that cools AI memory at the source NEO Semiconductor's revolutionary 3D X-DRAM for AI processors has passed proof-of-concept validation Angled view of the die stack, showing aligned data blocks and TSVs across layers. (Image credit: Intel)The second change is the interface. Rather than HBM's wide parallel PHY, XBM serializes data onto UCIe bundles at 32 GT/s, with the base die handling the serialize/deserialize step and routing all I/O to the compute die. Moving to a standard chiplet interconnect is what makes the design "chiplet-native" and, Intel argues, simpler and cheaper to package than an interposer-bound HBM stack. The tradeoff is that 32 GT/s is UCIe's current top data rate, so the interface is already running at the spec ceiling rather than leaving obvious headroom.Intel also leans heavily on repairability. The base die carries dedicated spare channels, built-in self-repair (BISR), decode and debug logic, and four sub-channels of redundant memory arrays that act as fungible spares for defects in the dies above — post-assembly repair designed to claw back yield on a very tall stack.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Base die floorplan labeling the UCIe block, BISR/decode/debug region, and spare channels for repair. (Image credit: Intel)A large portion of the patent application focuses not on the memory cell at all but on how to mount it. Intel details memory-on-package (MoP) and "reversed overhang" structures aimed at cutting the stack's Z-height — conventional MoP can add 300 to 350 micrometers (µm) — while removing the stiffener normally needed to control warpage and feeding DRAM power directly from the voltage regulator. This is the concrete basis for the "smaller, cheaper package" claim. Memory-on-package cross-section with die stacks flanking the SoC module (Image credit: Intel)XBM should not be confused with ZAM (Z-Angle Memory), the architecture Intel is co-developing with SoftBank subsidiary SAIMEMORY and set to present at the VLSI Symposium 2026. ZAM's innovation is on the bonding side — a fusion-bonded, nine-layer stack of largely conventional DRAM with roughly 3-µm-thin silicon between tiers — and it reportedly targets around twice HBM4's bandwidth density, with commercialization aimed at 2029. XBM, by contrast, is an Intel-only filing that changes the DRAM transistor itself and the interface. Read together, they suggest Intel is running at least two parallel HBM alternatives, a fitting move for a company that began in 1968 as a memory maker.The caveats on Intel’s proposed HBM architecture are the usual ones for a patent. The patent was filed 18 months ago, and there’s currently no product or roadmap, signaling potential intent rather than a shipping part. The UCIe interface is already at its rate ceiling, backend-transistor DRAM remains unproven at manufacturing scale, and the whole thing still has to justify itself against HBM4E and Intel's own ZAM timeline. history.author: Etiido Uko
history.titleZh: 英特尔专利揭示全新XBM内存架构，摒弃HBM昂贵硅中介层——后端晶体管DRAM堆叠采用UCIe连接与内置修复技术，缓解AI内存瓶颈
history.descriptionZh:
英特尔一项于2026年7月2日公开的专利申请（由Underfox曝光）揭示了该公司在高带宽内存（HBM）领域的新架构方案，旨在解决当前基于硅中介层的HBM在封装和成本上的瓶颈。该专利于2024年12月26日提交，描述了英特尔称之为“跨批次内存”（Cross-Batch Memory, XBM）的设计——一种“配备后道晶体管的超高带宽内存”，其目标是匹配HBM4的封装尺寸，但用后道（BEOL）晶体管和串行通用小芯片互连标准（UCIe）链路替代传统DRAM及其超宽接口。

英特尔提出的设计是一个内存堆叠架构，通过去掉昂贵的硅中介层并缩小封装体积，同时内置缺陷修复机制，从而解决传统HBM因组装成本过高的问题。专利申请中展示了一个包含内存堆叠和逻辑芯片、坐落于中介层上的封装横截面示意图。具体而言，该方案由多层内存芯片堆叠而成，每层芯片包含在后道工艺中制造的单晶体管单电容（1T1C）DRAM单元，通过硅通孔（TSV）“导流槽”和双面高带宽互连（HBI）连接。英特尔描述了每颗芯片容量约为1.5 GB——由768个“数据块”排列成32×24网格，分为8个通道，每个通道再分为8个子通道——典型堆叠高度为8层，并可扩展到16层。数据通过运行在32 GT/s（每秒千兆传输）的UCIe I/O束从堆叠中输出，经基die汇聚。

要理解英特尔的变革，需先回顾标准HBM的工作原理。HBM将DRAM芯片垂直堆叠在基die上，通过TSV互连，并利用极宽的并行接口（每堆叠约1024位）经由硅中介层与处理器通信。这种宽度赋予了HBM高带宽，但也导致封装昂贵且难以扩展，因为每一条导线都必须通过位于内存与计算芯片之间的中介层布线。随着AI加速器的性能增长远超内存供给速率，这一“内存墙”已成为性能的主导约束，因此几乎所有大型芯片制造商都在攻击接口和堆叠本身，而非逻辑单元。

XBM的第一个重大变化是结构性的。传统DRAM单元制造于前道（FEOL），即晶体管通常所在的基础硅层。而XBM将1T1C单元移至后道（BEOL）——晶体管层上方的金属和通孔堆叠层中，采用薄膜晶体管。在BEOL中构建内存使得英特尔能够将芯片划分为大量独立寻址的小内存块，这与英特尔一贯将内存直接置于逻辑上方的后道晶体管方向一致。

第二个变化是接口。不同于HBM的宽并行PHY，XBM将数据串行化为32 GT/s的UCIe束，由基die处理串并转换并路由所有I/O到计算芯片。采用标准小芯片互连使得设计成为“小芯片原生”的，英特尔认为这比基于中介层的HBM堆叠更简单、更便宜。代价是32 GT/s已是UCIe当前最高数据速率，因此接口已运行在规格上限，而非留有明显余量。

英特尔还高度重视可修复性。基die包含专用备用通道、内置自修复（BISR）、解码与调试逻辑，以及四个子通道的冗余内存阵列，作为上方芯片缺陷的通用备用——这是一种旨在从很高堆叠中挽回良率的组装后修复机制。专利中展示了基die的平面布局图，标注了UCIe模块、BISR/解码/调试区域以及用于修复的备用通道。

专利申请中很大篇幅并非聚焦内存单元本身，而是如何安装它。英特尔详细介绍了内存封装（Memory-on-Package, MoP）和“反向悬垂”结构，旨在降低堆叠的Z轴高度——传统MoP会增加300至350微米——同时去除通常用于控制翘曲的加强件，并直接从电压调节器为DRAM供电。这是“更小、更便宜封装”说法的具体依据。示意图显示了SoC模块两侧的内存die堆叠横截面。

XBM不应与ZAM（Z-Angle Memory）混淆，后者是英特尔与软银子公司SAIMEMORY合作开发、将在2026年VLSI研讨会上展示的架构。ZAM的创新在于键合侧——采用融合键合的九层堆叠，主要由传统DRAM构成，层间硅厚度约3微米——据称其带宽密度约为HBM4的两倍，计划2029年商业化。相比之下，XBM是英特尔独家的申请，更改了DRAM晶体管本身和接口。综合来看，这表明英特尔至少并行推进两条HBM替代路线，这对于一家1968年以内存制造商起家的公司来说是合理的举措。

对英特尔这一HBM架构的谨慎态度一如专利常见情况。该专利提交于18个月前，目前没有产品或路线图，表明这更多是意向而非成品。UCIe接口已达速率上限，后道晶体管DRAM尚未在制造规模上得到验证，整个方案仍需在HBM4E以及英特尔自己的ZAM时间表面前证明自己。
